item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very simple, but better for it. as someone as is very much a big fan of long, high concept stuff this is a big compliment. Photography added a lot, very atmospheric.

Characters feel very real and tangible even for as little as we saw them, the few minimal stories we see tell us a lot about their situation, and interaction. Whole thing feels oddly like a message in a bottle. Just a little piece of sad, self contained story I can do nothing about, except read and remember, knowing someone sent it out to be seen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Full marks in every respect.

It actually unclear to me whether the kids are aging or not, which might also be unclear to the foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm torn on this one. The Addendums are excellent, but the description is unclear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better! Thank you for taking care of that so quickly. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: alright, all done! pics have been shrunk to the size they appear in the article, and should be about 90% less murdery on data. thanks for bringing that to me attention :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fascinating and surreal. The fact that there's definitely an extradimensional space there, but they sort of… just don't want to leave, that's so different from a lot of similar things. If anything, this reminded me of the house that only Foundation personnel enter. :) This is good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm. I can't say that this is working for me. The description was unclear, so I had to reread it after reading the logs to make sense of it, and the overall atmosphere just doesn't gel with me quite right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is one of those rare situations where i feel that the gaps in the construction here work in its favor rather than against it. the situation that these people are in is ambiguous, their fate is ambiguous, the skip's actual mechanism is ambiguous. but it's couched in solemnity, and resentment, and helplessness, all of which are portrayed realistically and effectively and combine with the rest to make something haunting. this article isn't a stab in the chest, or a blow to the head, but an ache, and i think it's all the more effective for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well written and the photographs are great, but I feel like I'm missing something and I have no idea what it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The photography is absolutely beautiful and earned my upvote single-handedly. Upon reading the article, I wish there was a way to upvote something more than once.

Absolutely stupendous work. It's original, personal, and beautiful. What more can one ask?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the story this tells. It was a simple concept that you ran with, and it worked out well. The characters feel like characters and it left me satisfied when I was finished. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I present a very personal and somewhat bitter skip.

A big thanks to everyone who read/critted this, including Levi, Baubius, multizig, Cuppabro, rubberchickenzilla, and especially hippo.

All pictures were taken by myself.
